name: Small loop from Oloron-Sainte-Marie
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Oloron-Sainte-Marie are situated at the foot of the Pyrenees in southwestern France, offering a diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by its dramatic mountain landscapes, including iconic Pyrenean passes, rolling foothills, and river valleys. The confluence of the Gave d'Aspe and Gave d'Ossau rivers forms the Gave d'Oloron within the town, adding to the varied natural features. This area provides access to significant elevation gains and challenging climbs, alongside routes through agricultural lands and vineyards. Best road cycling routes around Oloron-Sainte-Marie The most popular road cycling route is Aubisque-Soulor – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees, a 51.2 miles (82.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through two legendary Pyrenean passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aubisque: The Tour Route, a difficult 50.7 miles (81.6 km) path. This route ascends the Col d'Aubisque, a climb known from professional cycling races. Local road cyclists also love the Loop 15: Port de Larrau and La Pierre Saint-Martin – Basque Country passes, a 63.8 miles (102.7 km) trail leading through high Pyrenean passes, often completed in about 6 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Oloron-Sainte-Marie is defined by challenging Pyrenean passes, scenic river valleys, and rolling Jurançon vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding mountain climbs to routes through the foothills. The routes in Oloron-Sainte-Marie are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 500 reviews. More than 28,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Oloron-Sainte-Marie's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: There are over 590 road cycling routes around Oloron-Sainte-Marie documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with approximately 75 easy, 300 moderate, and over 210 difficult options, many featuring significant Pyrenean climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: The region offers a diverse terrain, ranging from challenging mountain passes in the Pyrenees to rolling foothills, river valleys, and routes through agricultural lands and Jurançon vineyards. You'll encounter significant elevation gains on routes tackling legendary climbs, as well as more undulating paths through scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, while Oloron-Sainte-Marie is known for its challenging Pyrenean climbs, there are approximately 75 easy road cycling routes available. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and are suitable for those looking for a more relaxed ride or beginners.

Q: Which routes offer the most challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking significant challenges, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064636832/aubisque-soulor-emblematic-passes-of-the-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aubisque-Soulor – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154001512/loop-15-port-de-larrau-and-la-pierre-saint-martin-basque-country-passes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 15: Port de Larrau and La Pierre Saint-Martin – Basque Country passes&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gains and tackle iconic Pyrenean passes. Other demanding options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e850036722/aubisque-the-tour-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aubisque: The Tour Route&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068301174/marie-blanque-pass-emblematic-passes-of-the-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best times of year to go road cycling in Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: The region is ideal for road cycling from spring through autumn. Summer offers warm weather perfect for tackling high mountain passes, while spring and autumn provide milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially through the Jurançon vineyards and Pyrenean foothills. Always check local weather conditions, particularly for mountain routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the Oloron-Sainte-Marie area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e854692407/col-du-soudet-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col du Soudet Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e853005804/loop-along-the-green-of-arette" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop along the green of Arette&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and elevation profiles.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can experience the confluence of the Gave d'Aspe and Gave d'Ossau rivers in Oloron-Sainte-Marie itself, or ride through the picturesque Aspe and Ossau valleys with views of peaks like the Pic du Midi d'Ossau. Some routes also traverse the scenic Jurançon vineyards.

Q: Are there any famous Pyrenean passes accessible from Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: Absolutely. Oloron-Sainte-Marie is a gateway to several legendary Pyrenean passes, many of which are famous from the Tour de France. You can cycle to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aubisque&lt;/a&gt;, Col du Soulor, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt;, Col du Soudet, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Larrau&lt;/a&gt;, offering significant challenges and breathtaking views.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: The road cycling routes in Oloron-Sainte-Marie are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the dramatic mountain landscapes, the challenge of the Pyrenean passes, and the diverse scenery that includes river valleys and vineyards.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Basque Country passes from Oloron-Sainte-Marie?
A: Yes, some routes from Oloron-Sainte-Marie extend towards the Basque Country, allowing you to explore passes in that region. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154029794/loop-14-col-de-bagargui-from-mauleon-basque-country-passes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 14: Col de Bagargui from Mauléon – Basque Country passes&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging ride through high Pyrenean passes in the Basque Country.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see near the cycling routes?
A: Oloron-Sainte-Marie itself is rich in heritage. You can visit the UNESCO World Heritage &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme64.com/en/itinerary/oloron-sainte-marie/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sainte-Marie Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; with its remarkable 12th-century entrance, or explore the medieval Sainte-Croix district. The Promenade Bellevue also offers panoramic views of the town and the Pyrenees, which can be a great stop before or after a ride.

Q: Where can I find more information about the Oloron-Sainte-Marie region?
A: For more general information about Oloron-Sainte-Marie and the Béarn Pyrenees, you can visit the official tourism website at &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme64.com/en/our-destinations/welcome-to-bearn-pyrenees/oloron-sainte-marie/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;tourisme64.com&lt;/a&gt;. This can provide additional context for planning your cycling trip.
